title: Podman REST API can't create container with mounts
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I was trying to implement mounts handling in [podman-py project](https://github.com/containers/podman-py) but it is impossible to do it basing on REST API documentation.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Check REST API documentation https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/ContainerCreateLibpod (mounts option)

2. Run `curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock --header "Content-Type: application/json" --request POST --data '{"Image":"localhost/ubi8:latest","Name": "mushroom", "mounts":[{ "Target":"/dev/mushroom","Type":"tmpfs","TmpfsOptions":{"SizeBytes":100,"Mode":0}}]}' http://localhost/v4.0.2/libpod/containers/create`

**Describe the results you received:**

{"cause":"container directory cannot be empty","message":"container directory cannot be empty","response":500}

**Describe the results you expected:**

Container with proper tmpfs.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Ok so here's full story.
It looks like the documentation for [3.2 REST API ](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html?version=v3.2#operation/ContainerCreateLibpod) is valid for mount options (TmpfsOptions -> options, Target -> destination)
Proof : 

```
[root@localhost ~]# curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock --header "Content-Type: application/json" --request POST --data '{"Image":"localhost/ubi8:latest","Name": "snake","mounts":[{ "Destination":"/dev/snake","Type":"tmpfs","options":["size=100","mode=0"]}]}' http://localhost/v4.0.2/libpod/containers/create
{"Id":"522fc796090868e5121e194ed8b560e7179459dcf41a50dff5aa7128fc0b7fce","Warnings":[]}
[root@localhost ~]# podman inspect snake --format '{{json .HostConfig.Tmpfs}}'
{"/dev/snake":"size=100,mode=0,rw,rprivate,nosuid,nodev,tmpcopyup"}
```

I don't believe that's intentional.

So hopefully we can agree that REST API documentation is invalid ... or is it really ? 

```
[root@localhost ~]# curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock --header "Content-Type: application/json" --request POST --data '{"Image":"localhost/ubi8:latest","HostConfig":{"mounts":[{"Target":"/dev/badger","Type":"tmpfs","TmpfsOptions":{"SizeBytes":100,"Mode":0}}]}}' http://localhost/containers/create?name=badger
{"Id":"e9bda4cb9d47560c0d369f097829c9eccaaf9bf5452ea682987ac8bec58727cb","Warnings":[]}
[root@localhost ~]# podman inspect badger --format '{{json .HostConfig.Tmpfs}}'
{"/dev/badger":"size=100,mode=0,rw,rprivate,nosuid,nodev,tmpcopyup"}
```

It looks like the **other** endpoint actually can accept the 4.0 (or main) REST API documentation.
Please note the url difference : 
`http://localhost/v4.0.2/libpod/containers/create` vs `http://localhost/containers/create?name=badger`

I'm just a simple user of podman and I don't really understand what's the difference between these endpoints (and why there are 2 different endpoints (docker compatibility maybe?)).

Obviously same goes for any other mount type created that way.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@localhost ~]# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.17.7

Built:      Wed Mar  2 20:22:48 2022

```

comments.body:
The docs here are completely wrong - that is not the struct used. [This](https://github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/blob/main/specs-go/config.go#L110-L120) is what we're actually using.

@jwhonce I recall us seeing this before, is it fixed on newer versions?
I think the problem is that the types used in the swagger docs are not namespaced when they are generated so it will use the same struct name only once.

@msisj A quick note on the URL formatting, 

- URLs like `vX.Y.Z/libpod/containers` are for the podman/libpod API endpoints. 
- Whereas URLs like `vX.Y.Z/containers` or `containers` are for compatibility endpoints.

`/_ping` is an out-layer, it always returns the compatibility and libpod Headers. This was done to allow clients to switch between endpoints if they desire.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@jwhonce @Luap99 What should we do with this issue?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

posting an update to not leave this too quiet.

I tried to come up with a few solutions that basically were working but breaking changes.
The problem is simply to make the `swagger` understand that it should use our `spec.Mount` from the docs. Currently (and I **don't** know why) it seems impossible to redirect its attention to the right struct using `// swagger:model` or similar code comments
